"As droplets make the vast sea, let’s get greater impressions out of our small dreams."
Who we are

A commune of curious minds and caring hands

Palette is a youth-led collective born inside the classrooms of Development Studies at Calicut University — where theory meets the ground, and questions meet communities. We are not just volunteers; we are student-researchers, storytellers, and community companions.

We came together on 4 April 2024 — seven minds, one shared restlessness — to build something that sits at the intersection of academic inquiry and lived reality. Palette is our answer to the gap between what is studied and what is done.

01

Intervene

Design and deliver meaningful, ground-up programs for underserved communities with education, digital access, and skill-building at their core.

02

Inquire

Embed a research lens into every project. Ask why. Document how. Understand what the numbers never capture — the texture of lived experience.

03

Narrate

Use storytelling, film, and creative expression to bring those experiences into public consciousness. Make the invisible visible.

How we work

Research as intervention

What makes Palette distinct is not just what we do, but how we think about what we do. Every project is designed with three interlocking layers.

Layer I
○

Community Entry

We do not arrive with fixed answers. We arrive with open questions. Before any program is designed, we listen, observe, and learn the shape of the community — its rhythms, its needs, its existing strengths.

Layer II
◍

Action with Inquiry

While programs run, we simultaneously document, observe, and ask. What is changing? What are communities telling us through their participation and through their silences?

Layer III
●

Reflection & Narrative

After every intervention, we turn inward. We process what we witnessed and produce written reflections, stories, and documented research outputs. Palette is a thinking organisation.

Our work on the ground

Projects that matter

01Project Roots — Wayanad
02Project THARI — Thenjhipalam
April 27–28, 2024 · Phase I

VACARE — Into the Roots

A two-day residential vacation camp for tribal school students in Pulpally, Wayanad. Sessions blended academic engagement with play-based learning, creative expression, and genuine human connection.

Tribal EducationPlay-based Learning
August 17, 2024 · Phase II

VACARE 2.0 — Return & Follow-through

Building on relationships formed in Phase 1, Vacare 2.0 focused on distributing school kits to ensure tribal children enter the academic year with dignity. In collaboration with Kites India. Community trust is built in return.

School KitsKites India
Documentary Film

Enne Malaithama

"What the mountain does not forget" — a short film capturing what data cannot: the faces, voices, and quiet moments that defined this intervention. Palette’s first act of research through cinema.

▶ Watch Film
Inaugurated August 21, 2024

B4Bold — Digital Literacy Drive

An ongoing initiative targeting MGNREGA workers — a community that sustains rural India’s infrastructure yet remains excluded from digital transitions. Inaugurated by writer Manjuvaikhari at Thenjhipalam AUP School.

OngoingMGNREGA Workers
Research Question

Beyond Skills — The Real Barriers

What does digital inclusion mean for communities whose relationship with the state is mediated by bureaucracy and exclusion? What barriers — beyond skills — keep rural workers offline? This inquiry runs through every session.

Participatory ResearchDigital Divide
Coming Soon

Something new is being mixed on the palette

Our next intervention is being shaped — informed by what we have learned, questioned, and unlearned across our first year. Watch this space.

From the field

Moments that stayed

Photography as testimony — evidence of presence, of care, of what happens when you stay long enough for trust to form.

Our roots

Core Support from the Department

Palette grew from the intellectual soil of the Department of Development Studies, University of Calicut. These are the friends, colleagues, and fellow-travellers from the department who walked beside us — challenging us in seminars, joining us in the field, and believing in the project when it was still just a conversation.

Department of Development Studies · University of Calicut
